definition: Represent each sample by a set of biological entities, such as drug targets, disease genes, pathway members, or perturbation targets. Compute shortest-path proximity between sets on a biological interaction network and transform distance into similarity. For SPECTRA, this audits whether evaluation examples are close to training examples in network medicine space.
example_usage: python network_set_proximity_similarity.py --edges interactome.csv --train train_sets.csv --eval eval_sets.csv --out pairwise_similarity.csv --set-col targets --scale 2.0

citations.title: Network-based in silico drug efficacy screening

evidence.summary: The paper introduces drug-disease proximity between drug targets and disease proteins in the interactome.
It compares shortest-path, closest, kernel, center, and separation measures and reports proximity-based similarity for repurposing.

limitations: Coverage and bias in the interaction network can strongly affect distances.
Shortest-path proximity does not distinguish activation, inhibition, tissue context, or causal direction.
Entity annotations are often incomplete and should not be treated as a complete mechanism.
quality_gates: Report network source, version, directedness, and disconnected-node handling.
Report whether proximity is raw shortest-path distance, closest distance, z-scored against random sets, or another statistic.
Check sensitivity to high-degree nodes and to the number of entities in each set.
